q: A spectrometer is a scientific instrument used to combine and synthesize spectral components of a physical phenomenon.
A spectrometer is a widely used scientific tool for many disciplines, including biology, chemistry, and agriculture.
The optical spectrometer consists of a light source, collimator, dispersion elements and a magnifying glass.
The dispersion element in an optical spectrometer can be a prism or a grating.
The angle of dispersion determined by a spectrometer is used to identify the temperature of the light source.
In a spectrophotometer, a dispersion element separates light into spectrum which is allowed to fall on the sample to be investigated.
The change in the absorption and emission of the light intensity with wavelength allows for the identification of the materials.
UV-Vis spectrometer, spectrofluorometer, and Raman spectrometer are three of the most common optical spectrometers.
In a spectrophotometer, the dispersion element is used to focus all light onto the sample without separation.
